name: FDPF Pending Reason
description: Leads that failed their first draft payment due to missing or pending reasons. These require immediate investigation to resolve the payment issue.

name: FDPF Insufficient Funds
description: Leads that failed their first draft payment due to insufficient funds (NSF). These require immediate investigation to resolve the payment issue.

name: FDPF Incorrect Banking Info
description: Leads that failed their first draft payment due to wrong account information. These require immediate investigation to resolve the payment issue.

name: FDPF Unauthorized Draft
description: Payments failed because the bank did not authorize the draft. Requires a three-way call with the bank and client to authorize before redating the policy.

name: Pending Failed Payment Fix
description: Indicates an agent has requested a policy redate or redraft following a failed payment. Leads move back to the customer pipeline if successful or return to FDPF if they fail again.

name: Pending Lapse
description: Clients who previously made payments but have now missed a subsequent premium. Urgent action is needed to update billing or request a redraft to prevent a full chargeback.

name: Chargeback Failed Payment
description: Policies that are fully cancelled because a redraft or billing update wasn't completed in time. A brand new application is required if the client wants coverage again.

name: Chargeback Cancellation
description: Leads who requested cancellation or were cancelled by the carrier before commissions were received. Agents must contact these clients to determine the reason and attempt a new sale.

name: Pending Chargeback Fix
description: Tracks the original charged-back policy (with negative value) while a new fix policy has been sold. It remains here until the new policy drafts successfully or also charges back.

name: Chargeback Fixed
description: The negative value of a previous chargeback is moved here once a new fix policy is successfully paying premiums. This allows the agency to track recovered value.

name: Issued - Pending First Draft
description: Leads approved by the carrier that are waiting for the first premium payment to process. The wait time varies by carrier, ranging from 24 hours to 4 business days.

name: Premium Paid - Commission Pending
description: The client has successfully paid their first premium, but the agency has not yet received the commission payment. This tracks the gap between client payment and agency revenue.

name: ACTIVE PLACED - Paid as Earned
description: Policies that pay commissions as they are earned that have successfully completed their first draft. This stage marks the beginning of the active policy lifecycle.

name: ACTIVE PLACED - Paid as Advanced
description: Policies that pay commissions in advance that have successfully completed their first draft. The client is now considered active and placed within the system.

name: ACTIVE - 3 Months +
description: Tracks active clients who have consistently paid their premiums for 3 months. This monitors long-term policy retention and stability.

name: ACTIVE - 6 months +
description: Tracks active clients who have consistently paid their premiums for 6 months. This monitors long-term policy retention and stability.

name: ACTIVE - 9 months
description: Tracks active clients who have consistently paid their premiums for 9 months. This monitors long-term policy retention and stability.

name: ACTIVE - Past Charge-Back Period
description: Clients who have maintained their policy long enough to move beyond the carrier's chargeback window. These represent fully secured commissions for the agency.

name: Chargeback DQ
description: Customers who have caused multiple chargebacks within the agency. This stage serves to flag high-risk individuals to prevent further loss.

name: Transfer API
description: Initial landing spot for new leads from Jotform submissions. Opportunities stay here until the end of the day when they are categorized and noted.

name: Chargeback Fix API
description: Entry point for new Jotform submissions specifically related to customers with existing chargeback issues. Leads are moved to specific categories with added notes at the end of each day.

name: Incomplete Transfer
description: Used for calls that were never successfully transferred or dropped before the agent could confirm required details. This tracks leads that need further initial contact.

name: Returned To Center - DQ
description: Leads that are eligible for a policy but the agency is not interested in selling to them. These are Orbit Insurance disqualifications and will not be accepted if re-transferred.

name: Previously Sold BPO
description: Existing clients with a policy where the initial draft date has not yet passed. Notes are added to schedule a follow-up call for additional coverage one week after the draft date.

name: DQ'd Can't be sold
description: Fully disqualified leads that the agency believes should not be contacted or sold a policy by anyone. This represents a total exclusion from the sales funnel.

name: Needs LA Callback
description: Leads that couldn't be submitted because essential information, like SSN or banking details, was missing. These require a callback to finalize the application.

name: Application Withdrawn
description: Submitted applications that were withdrawn by the carrier for reasons like max coverage or too many prior attempts. Specific internal notes will explain the reason for each withdrawal.

name: Declined Underwriting
description: Leads that have been declined by all current carriers available to the agency. These may be revisited in the future if new carrier options become available.

name: Pending Approval
description: The holding stage for applicants after an application has been submitted to a carrier. Leads remain here until the carrier provides an approval decision.

name: Pending Manual Action
description: Applications that are paused because the carrier requires more information, such as form corrections or a phone interview. Actions must be completed before the carrier continues investigation.

name: GI DQ
description: GI Qualified Customers

name: TCPA
description: TCPA list
